Name: cuda_kernel_optimization
Title: Optimizing CUDA Kernels for Low-Latency Large Language Model Inference
Short Hypothesis: Carefully optimized CUDA kernels can significantly reduce the inference latency of large language models without sacrificing accuracy. By analyzing the performance bottlenecks of existing kernels and applying targeted optimizations, we can improve the overall efficiency of LLM inference.
Related Work: Recent works such as FlashSVD, SwiftSpec, and Hybrid JIT-CUDA Graph Optimization have demonstrated the potential for optimizing CUDA kernels and inference pipelines for large language models. However, these approaches often focus on specific aspects of optimization, such as memory efficiency or compute utilization, and may not provide a comprehensive solution for low-latency inference. Our proposal builds upon these works and aims to provide a more holistic approach to optimizing CUDA kernels for LLM inference.
Abstract: This proposal presents a novel approach to optimizing CUDA kernels for low-latency large language model inference. By analyzing the performance bottlenecks of existing kernels and applying targeted optimizations, we aim to reduce the inference latency of LLMs without sacrificing accuracy. Our approach involves optimizing memory access patterns, reducing kernel launch overhead, and improving compute utilization. We will evaluate our optimized kernels on a range of LLM models and benchmark datasets, using metrics such as tokens per second, latency, and energy efficiency. Our goal is to provide a comprehensive solution for low-latency LLM inference that can be applied in practice.
Experiments: Optimize memory access patterns by reordering kernel launches and reducing memory allocation overhead
Reduce kernel launch overhead by fusing kernels and minimizing synchronization points
Improve compute utilization by optimizing thread block configuration and register allocation
Evaluate optimized kernels on a range of LLM models and benchmark datasets, using metrics such as tokens per second, latency, and energy efficiency
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of LLM models and CUDA kernels may make it challenging to identify and optimize performance bottlenecks
The optimized kernels may not generalize well to different LLM models or hardware platforms
The evaluation metrics used may not capture all aspects of performance, and additional metrics may be needed to provide a comprehensive understanding of the optimized kernels
